user_query: I’m a ballplayer who’s great on the field but not so great with the math or wording. Please implement a single script (standard library only; your choice of Python or Node) that reads my raw files and produces three outputs I can send to my coach and agent. Inputs are in input/games_week_2024-04.csv and input/draft_notes.txt. Write outputs to: out/weekly_stats.json, out/weekly_update.md, and out/rewritten_messages.json. Requirements: 1) Compute weekly batting stats from the CSV: totals for games, at_bats, hits, doubles, triples, home_runs, walks, hbp, sac_flies, rbi, runs, stolen_bases, strikeouts, and total_bases. Use: singles = hits − (doubles + triples + home_runs); total_bases = singles*1 + doubles*2 + triples*3 + home_runs*4. Rates: batting_average = hits/at_bats; obp = (hits + walks + hbp)/(at_bats + walks + hbp + sac_flies); slg = total_bases/at_bats; ops = obp + slg. Round batting_average, obp, slg, and ops to 3 decimal places in all outputs. 2) Rewrite the messages in input/draft_notes.txt for clarity, brevity, and a friendly professional tone, preserving facts and intent, removing slang and apologies. Word limits: weekly_update ≤ 120 words; coach_dm ≤ 80 words. 3) Compose a weekly report at out/weekly_update.md with: Title line “Weekly Update: <min_date> to <max_date>” (dates from the CSV); an Intro section that is the rewritten weekly_update message; a Stats Summary section listing BA, OBP, SLG, OPS, and the integer totals (AB, H, HR, 2B, 3B, BB, HBP, SF, R, RBI, SB, K); and a Next Week Focus section containing exactly three concise, action-oriented bullets derived from the first three items under the === focus === section of input/draft_notes.txt (rewrite each bullet to ≤ 12 words). 4) Write out/weekly_stats.json with this structure: { "totals": {"games": int, "at_bats": int, "hits": int, "doubles": int, "triples": int, "home_runs": int, "walks": int, "hbp": int, "sac_flies": int, "rbi": int, "runs": int, "stolen_bases": int, "strikeouts": int, "total_bases": int}, "rates": {"batting_average": number, "obp": number, "slg": number, "ops": number}, "per_game": [ {"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "opponent": string, "at_bats": int, "hits": int, "walks": int, "hbp": int, "sac_flies": int, "doubles": int, "triples": int, "home_runs": int, "runs": int, "rbi": int, "stolen_bases": int, "strikeouts": int, "total_bases": int} ... ] }. 5) Write out/rewritten_messages.json with keys weekly_update and coach_dm containing the rewritten texts. 6) Cross-check: the Stats Summary numbers in out/weekly_update.md must exactly match the values in out/weekly_stats.json (same rounding). Don’t use the Internet or external data; only these inputs. Make sure every required file path exists on completion. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
